card1.lv1_text: It is the brain and heart of this university, housing both a vast sea of books and the wisdom of decision-making.
card1.lv2_text: No need to delve deep into the maze’s core. Head to the North Campus, where you’ll find the entrance.

card2.lv3_text: At the the north gate of the Central Building (across from FB)

freshman.question: Looking for a snack or daily essentials? Which floor is the 'Youme Supermarket' located on?
options: A. 2nd Floor (2F)
B. 1st Floor (1F)
C. Ground Floor (GF)
D. Basement (-1F)
freshman.successMessage: Correct! You found the survival hub. The regional clue is revealed!

freshman.question: Feeling stressed? Which 2nd-floor service offers 6 free counseling sessions for students?
options: A. IT Support Desk
B. One-Stop Service Center
C. Student Counselling Centre
D. Admissions Office
freshman.successMessage: Spot on! Health is wealth. The final location is now visible!

B. 1st Floor (1F)
C. 2nd Floor (2F)
D. Basement (-1F)
senior.successMessage: Senior level knowledge! You know the secret shortcuts. Level 2 clue unlocked!

options: A. A music score
B. Wired headphones
C. A student ID card
D. A permission slip
senior.successMessage: Exactly! Quiet practice is key. The final coordinates are revealed!

visitor.question: The iconic 'holed' design of this building is inspired by which element of Suzhou gardens?
options: A. Bamboo leaves
B. Taihu Stone (Scholar's Stone)
C. Silk umbrellas
D. Traditional fans
visitor.successMessage: Correct! It represents the integration of knowledge. Level 2 clue unlocked!

visitor.question: CB features many open sofas and 'sliced' learning zones. Which XJTLU philosophy does this represent?
options: A. Silent-only learning
B. Student-Centered Education
C. Faculty-only access
D. Traditional lecture-style
visitor.successMessage: Perfect! You've grasped our core values. The final secret is yours!

card1.lv1_text: A complex of laboratories and futuristic classrooms where scientific equations meet architectural grandeur. Often dubbed the 'bridge of logic'.
card1.lv2_text: Situated in the heart of South Campus, its unique geometric design features multiple interconnected blocks and distinctive sky bridges.

card2.lv3_text: Walk up the main stairs of Block B to the 2nd floor open study corner. The magic tag hides behind the third whiteboard facing the corridor!

freshman.question: What is the primary focus of the facilities in Science Building B?
options: A. Liberal arts classrooms
B. Advanced laboratories and science research
C. Indoor sports courts
D. University administration
freshman.successMessage: Correct! Your scientific intuition clears the path—the location is narrowed down.

D. Bamboo archways

card1.lv1_text: A silent treasury of time and memory. Here, the university's past, present, and future artifacts are carefully preserved.
card1.lv2_text: Find this cultural epicenter near the southern entrance of the North Campus, just steps away from the central administrative block.

card2.lv3_text: Enter the main exhibition hall, head past the scale model of the campus, and check under the showcase describing the university's founding year!

freshman.question: What is the primary function of the XJTLU Museum?
options: A. Hosting e-sports tournaments
B. Archiving and showcasing the university's history and milestones
C. Teaching culinary arts
D. Storing lost and found items
freshman.successMessage: Correct! You unlocked a piece of history—the regional clue is revealed!

freshman.successMessage: Excellent! The timeline is complete, and the precise NFC location is yours!

card1.lv1_text: A vibrant sanctuary where espresso shots fuel endless club discussions, late-night cram sessions, and serendipitous encounters.
card1.lv2_text: Nestled in the student activity center area, this spot is synonymous with the lively, informal learning culture of the campus.

card2.lv3_text: Order a latte, walk to the high tables by the right-side window, and peek underneath the table closest to the door to find the tag!

freshman.question: What role does Hui Bar primarily serve for students at XJTLU?
options: A. A quiet exam-only zone
B. A social hub for informal learning, discussions, and networking
C. The main faculty dining hall
D. A silent library
freshman.successMessage: Exactly! The aroma of coffee leads you closer to the truth.

freshman.question: 'Informal learning' is a key concept at XJTLU. Which of these activities best exemplifies informal learning at Hui Bar?

D. Registering for upcoming semester courses
freshman.successMessage: Brilliant! You've mastered the art of informal learning. The exact tag location is now unblurred!

card1.lv1_text: A place of first steps and steady routines, where many students begin their campus journey among classrooms, corridors, and quiet preparation.
card1.lv2_text: Head toward the Foundation Building area on campus. Look for the side facing the Central Building, where students often pass through between classes.

card2.lv3_text: Follow the final route images to the exact spot in Foundation Building. The NFC tag is placed at the clearly marked position shown in the last clue image.

freshman.question: As a new student, what would you most likely use Foundation Building for in your first weeks on campus?
options: A. Attending regular classes and getting used to campus learning routines
B. Visiting a major sports venue for team training
C. Exploring historical exhibitions about the university
D. Entering restricted scientific labs for advanced experiments
freshman.successMessage: Correct! You're thinking like a newcomer. The next clue now points you closer to Foundation Building.

freshman.question: Which scene would make a freshman most confident they have arrived at Foundation Building?
options: A. Display cases introducing the university's history
B. Corridors with classrooms and students heading between lessons
C. Rows of workout machines and indoor courts
D. A social cafe atmosphere with drinks and casual chats
freshman.successMessage: Exactly. You picked the most recognizable study scene, and the final NFC route is now revealed.

senior.question: For senior students, what makes Foundation Building stand out from more specialized campus spaces?
options: A. It supports broad day-to-day teaching rather than niche research or sports functions
B. It mainly hosts varsity competitions and fitness sessions
C. It focuses on preserving archives and campus heritage
D. It is intended mostly for exhibition visitors instead of students
senior.successMessage: Correct. That broader academic role is the key pattern, and Level 2 is now unlocked.

senior.question: Which flow of movement is most typical around Foundation Building during a normal class day?
options: A. Students rotating between lectures, classrooms, and short preparation breaks
B. Athletes arriving for drills and court bookings
C. Visitors quietly moving through curated exhibits
D. Researchers entering controlled lab spaces
senior.successMessage: Exactly. That everyday class-flow gives it away, and the exact NFC position is now visible.

visitor.question: If you were visiting campus for the first time, how would you best understand Foundation Building?
options: A. A core teaching building tied to introductory learning and everyday classes
B. A performance hall mainly used for ceremonies and concerts
C. A recreation center focused on sports and training
D. A heritage museum preserving institutional memory
visitor.successMessage: Correct. You identified its public-facing role well, and the regional clue is now clearer.

B. It is best known for preserving university artifacts and timelines
C. It is dominated by sports courts and physical training facilities
D. It mainly serves coffee and social gatherings
visitor.successMessage: Exactly. That visitor-friendly description points to the right building, and the final route is unlocked.

card1.lv1_text: A place where motion becomes habit and effort echoes through courts, studios, and training rooms all day long.
card1.lv2_text: Head toward the campus sports complex. Look for the building known for workouts, indoor courts, and student training sessions.

card2.lv3_text: Follow the final route images outside the GYM to reach the exact NFC position. The tag is placed at the clearly marked spot shown in the last clue image.

options: A. Join workouts, sports practice, or general physical training
B. Read archives about the university's past
C. Attend most of your daily academic lectures
D. Meet friends mainly for drinks and casual conversation
freshman.successMessage: Correct! That active first impression leads you closer to the right sports building.

freshman.question: Which detail would make a new student immediately think, 'This must be the GYM'?
options: A. Indoor courts, workout spaces, and people doing sports activities
B. Long corridors of classrooms and lecture rooms
C. Quiet exhibitions telling the campus story
D. A cafe-style area built around drinks and chatting

C. A cultural landmark used for exhibitions and archives
D. A mainly administrative hub for paperwork and services

D. A social area centered on food and drinks
